vocab.definition: Changes in a population over time.
Process where organisms better suited to their environment survive and reproduce more successfully.
A measure of an individual's ability to survive and reproduce in its environment.
A group of organisms that can interbreed and produce fertile offspring in nature.
The incidence of a gene variant in a population, expressed as a number from 0-1.
All of the alleles for a specific gene in a population.
The different traits or alleles already present in a population.
A trait that helps an organism survive and reproduce in its environment.
Intentional breeding for certain traits by humans (e.g., dog breeds, crops).
Likelihood of passing on a trait based on attractiveness or ability to defeat rivals for mating.
When pathogens develop the ability to defeat the drugs designed to kill them.
A diagram showing evolutionary relationships between species.
The place on a phylogenetic tree where a branch splits, representing a common ancestor.
Acronym for the conditions needed for evolution: Variation, Inheritance, Selection, Time, Adaptation.
Selection that favors one of the extreme phenotypes, shifting the bell curve in one direction.
Selection that favors intermediate phenotypes, narrowing the bell curve.
Selection that favors both extreme phenotypes, creating two peaks in the bell curve.
The formation of new and distinct species in the course of evolution.
Lamarck's incorrect idea that organisms can pass on traits acquired during their lifetime to offspring.
Able to reproduce and have offspring.
